description: Split `task-mgr init --from-json X` into project-level `task-mgr init` (no PRD; scaffolds .task-mgr/, runs migrations, writes default config) and PRD-level `task-mgr loop init X` / `task-mgr batch init <glob>...`. The current `init --from-json X` form survives indefinitely as a deprecated shim. Add a new `task-mgr enhance` command that writes/updates a marker-fenced workflow block in CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md so agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Aider) know how to use task-mgr correctly. `task-mgr init --enhance` is a convenience flag combining both. Loop and Batch top-level commands become parent-with-subcommand (Init/Run) with backwards-compat shim for the flat form.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output (scoped to changed files per iteration; full at REVIEW-001)
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: All scoped tests pass with `cargo test -p task-mgr <module>` per iteration; full `cargo test` at REVIEW-001
Rust: `cargo run --bin gen-docs -- --check` passes after FEAT-006 (CI gate)

No breaking changes to existing `commands::init::init()` signature â€” its callers double after this PR (top-level Init shim + LoopCommand::Init + BatchCommand::Init), so any signature change is a refactor that must keep all three call sites compiling.
Pattern: handle Result/Option explicitly â€” no unwrap() in production paths. Use `map_err(TaskMgrError::...)` for DB calls.
Pattern: file writes go through atomic helpers â€” `marker_splice::write_atomic` for enhance, `write_default_model`'s tempfile pattern for config.
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN â€” Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION â€” ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE â€” Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS â€” Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY â€” Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
6. POLISH â€” Docs, formatting, minor improvements
principles: Quality dimensions explicit â€” qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation â€” prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations (1:10+ savings ratio)
Edge cases = test cases â€” every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 â€” REVIEW must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Indefinite shim â€” the deprecated `task-mgr init --from-json X` MUST keep working byte-for-byte after this PR; the canonical form is documented but the shim is permanent. Plan Â§11.

prohibitedOutcomes: Removing or changing the shim's row-level behavior â€” `init --from-json X --append --update-existing` MUST produce the same DB state as `loop init X --append --update-existing`. This is enforced by a golden-file equivalence test in FEAT-005.
Touching any PRD JSON file from the new project-level `init_project` function â€” that side effect belongs to `loop init` / `batch init` only.

Auto-firing the interactive model picker from `loop init` or `batch init` â€” picker is project-level config and fires only from `task-mgr init` (project-level) and from the deprecated `init --from-json X` shim path.
Adding a `--force` flag to project-level `task-mgr init` â€” project-level reset is `rm -rf .task-mgr/`. Reject `--force` with empty `from_json` and exit 2 with a clear stderr message.
Hand-editing `.claude/commands/tasks.md` â€” it is regenerated by `cargo run --bin gen-docs`.

Removing pattern matches over `Commands::Loop { .. }` / `Commands::Batch { .. }` from non-dispatch sites and assuming the old fields persist â€” Learning [1069]: every exhaustive match must be updated to the new variant shape.
Using `std::fs::write` in the new `enhance` module â€” all writes go through `marker_splice::write_atomic` (FEAT-001) to avoid torn writes.
Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content.
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring).

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `src/util/marker_splice.rs` exists with `pub fn splice_block(...)` and `pub fn write_atomic(...)`
Positive: `src/bin/gen-docs.rs` imports both functions from `task_mgr::util::marker_splice` (grep confirms no local duplicates remain)
Unit test: splice_block on empty input + replacement â†’ result contains markers wrapping the replacement
Unit test: splice_block on existing block â†’ byte-for-byte replacement, content outside markers unchanged
Unit test: splice_block called twice with same args â†’ idempotent (second call's output == first call's output)
Unit test: write_atomic writes via tempfile + rename in same dir; failure-to-persist case leaves target file unchanged (use a permission-denied or read-only-dir simulation)
Existing gen-docs tests pass after the move: `cargo test --bin gen-docs` (or wherever the tests live post-move)
CI gate: `cargo run --bin gen-docs -- --check` exits 0 (no diff in `.claude/commands/tasks.md` from the refactor)
Negative: must NOT use `std::fs::write` in `marker_splice.rs` â€” all writes through tempfile pattern
Negative: must NOT change `splice_block`'s public API after this task lands â€” FEAT-003 will consume it with the same signature

CONTRACT: `splice_block` signature is `fn(current: &str, marker_begin: &str, marker_end: &str, replacement: &str) -> String`. FEAT-003 wires `<!-- TASK_MGR:BEGIN -->` / `<!-- TASK_MGR:END -->` through these params.
userStories.description:
Move `splice_block` and the atomic-write helper from `src/bin/gen-docs.rs` (lines 177-214) into a new shared module `src/util/marker_splice.rs`. Update gen-docs.rs to import from the new location. This unblocks FEAT-003 (enhance) which will reuse both for managing the `<!-- TASK_MGR:BEGIN/END -->` block in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md.

DO:
- Create `src/util/marker_splice.rs` with `pub fn splice_block(current: &str, marker_begin: &str, marker_end: &str, replacement: &str) -> String` and `pub fn write_atomic(path: &Path, content: &str) -> io::Result<()>`.
- Wire the module into `src/util/mod.rs` (create if it doesn't exist) and re-export from `src/lib.rs` if the binary needs `task_mgr::util::marker_splice::...`.
- Update `src/bin/gen-docs.rs` to call the new module instead of local copies; preserve existing call sites and tests byte-for-byte.
- Move (or re-add at the new location) the existing `test_splice_is_idempotent` test so coverage doesn't regress.

DO NOT:
- Change `splice_block`'s behavior. Marker params are arguments so the same fn serves `<!-- MODELS:BEGIN -->` (gen-docs) and `<!-- TASK_MGR:BEGIN -->` (enhance).
- Hardcode marker strings inside `splice_block`.
- Break `cargo run --bin gen-docs -- --check` â€” it must still pass after the refactor.

Edge cases:
- File has no marker block yet â†’ match current gen-docs behavior (typically: append the block; verify what gen-docs actually does and preserve it).
- File has marker_begin but no marker_end â†’ return Err or current gen-docs behavior (verify).
- Multiple marker pairs â†’ replace first; preserve current gen-docs semantics.

Known-bad to guard against:
- A naive impl that overwrites the whole file on splice â€” must preserve everything outside the markers.
- Using `std::fs::write` directly instead of atomic tempfile+rename â€” torn writes on crash leave partial files.
edgeCases: File has no marker block: append at end (matches current gen-docs behavior)
File has unbalanced markers (begin without end, or vice versa): return Err with file:position context
Multiple marker pairs in same file: replace first; current gen-docs behavior is the spec
Write to read-only directory: write_atomic returns Err; target file unchanged

failureModes.cause: Tempfile rename across filesystems (e.g. tmpfile in /tmp, target in /home)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Use same-directory tempfile (parent of target); rename is atomic within filesystem

failureModes.cause: gen-docs CI check fails post-refactor due to subtle splice_block behavior drift
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Bisect against pre-refactor splice_block; preserve exact semantics; do not 'improve' the function

userStories.notes:
Source code to move: `src/bin/gen-docs.rs:177-214` (splice_block + write_atomic). Existing test pattern: gen-docs.rs:244 (`test_splice_is_idempotent`). Reuse pattern from `src/loop_engine/project_config.rs:215-221` (tempfile create + persist same-dir) â€” that's the production atomic-write idiom in this codebase.

Learning [1093]: idempotent guard pattern â€” start with a symlink check before any file write. Apply to write_atomic.

Learning [1075]: SetupFix struct pattern (name/description/applied/error) â€” not directly applicable here (no result struct needed for a low-level util), but informs FEAT-003's EnhanceResult.
qualityDimensions: splice_block byte-for-byte preserves content outside markers (regression test ensures)
write_atomic never leaves partial files on crash (tempfile + rename pattern)
Public API stable after this task â€” FEAT-003 will call into it without re-negotiation
userStories.title: Extract shared marker-splice util from gen-docs

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `init_project(fresh_tmpdir)` creates `.task-mgr/tasks.db` and `.task-mgr/config.json` with v1+ defaults

Unit test: init_project on tmpdir with existing config.json containing `{"customField": "keepme"}` â†’ after call, customField is still "keepme"; defaults filled in for missing keys (use the project_config.rs value-roundtrip pattern)
Unit test: idempotency â€” call init_project twice; second call returns InitResult with `created_dirs: false`, `created_config: false`; file contents byte-identical between calls
Unit test: tmpdir with pre-existing `tasks/foo.json` â†’ init_project does NOT read foo.json; foo.json byte-identical before/after (use mtime + content hash assertion)
Unit test: tmpdir with empty `.task-mgr/` (no DB, no config) â†’ init_project creates both; subsequent calls are no-ops
Unit test: non-TTY environment (override via IsTerminal injection or env var) â†’ picker not invoked; init_project succeeds with no defaultModel set
Negative: grep `src/commands/init/mod.rs::init_project` for `parse_prd`, `prefix`, `taskPrefix`, `tasks/` â€” none of these tokens appear in the function body or any helper it calls
Negative: must NOT create `tasks/` subdirectory
Negative: must NOT modify the existing `pub fn init(...)` (PRD-import) signature or behavior â€” gauntlet: `git diff` on the existing init() body shows zero changes after this task
CONTRACT: InitResult variant or struct extension compiles against the existing `output_result(&result, cli.format)` call in main.rs â€” verify by running `cargo check` from main.rs's perspective
Failure mode: if open_and_migrate fails (e.g., permission denied), init_project returns Err(TaskMgrError::...) with the underlying cause; no partial state in .task-mgr/
userStories.description:
Add `pub fn init_project(dir: &Path) -> TaskMgrResult<InitResult>` in `src/commands/init/mod.rs`. Project-level only: ensures `.task-mgr/` exists, runs migrations, writes a default `.task-mgr/config.json` preserving any existing keys, fires the model picker only if stdin+stderr are both TTYs. Idempotent. Does NOT read or write any PRD JSON.

The existing `pub fn init(...)` (the PRD-import function, currently at src/commands/init/mod.rs:182) stays exactly as-is â€” FEAT-005 will call it from the shim path and FEAT-004 will call it from LoopCommand::Init / BatchCommand::Init.

DO:
- Reuse `open_and_migrate(dir)` from `src/db/connection.rs:58` for DB scaffolding + migrations.
- Reuse `ensure_directories(db_dir)` from `src/loop_engine/env.rs` for `.task-mgr/` subdir scaffolding.
- Reuse the `serde_json::Value`-based read-mutate-write pattern from `write_default_model` (`src/loop_engine/project_config.rs:180-221`) so existing config keys (including unknown ones the user added) are preserved.
- Fire the model picker via the existing helper in `src/commands/models/ensure_default.rs` only when both stdin and stderr are TTYs (non-TTY: skip silently per CLAUDE.md line 63).
- Return an InitResult variant or extend the existing struct with fields like `created_dirs`, `created_config`, `migrations_applied` so the CLI can print a useful summary.

DO NOT:
- Touch any PRD JSON file. Do not call `parse_prd`, do not glob `tasks/`, do not invoke prefix write-back logic.
- Accept a `--force` flag â€” project-level reset is `rm -rf .task-mgr/` per Plan Â§6.
- Print the picker prompt when not a TTY â€” silent skip.
- Hard-fail if `.task-mgr/` already exists â€” that's the idempotent re-init path.

Edge cases (each must have a test):
- Fresh tmpdir (no .task-mgr/) â†’ create everything; config has v1+ defaults.
- Existing config.json with user-set fields (e.g. `{"customField": "x", "ollamaUrl": "http://custom"}`) â†’ preserve ALL existing fields; only fill in absent defaults.
- Existing DB at older schema â†’ migrations run cleanly; no data loss.
- Non-TTY environment (e.g. test harness) â†’ picker skipped silently.
- `tasks/foo.json` exists in CWD â†’ byte-identical before/after init_project.

Known-bad to guard against:
- A naive impl that overwrites config.json wholesale, dropping unknown user-set keys (would pass a test that only checks `defaultModel` but is wrong).
- An impl that creates `tasks/` directory â€” that's owned by loop init / batch init.
edgeCases: Fresh tmpdir: create .task-mgr/ + DB + config
Existing config with unknown user fields: preserve those fields verbatim
Older-schema DB: migrations apply; no data loss
Non-TTY: skip picker silently
tasks/*.json present in CWD: do not read or touch

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Return Err(TaskMgrError::...) with cause; .task-mgr/ may be partial but no PRD data corrupted (no PRD ever read)
Tempfile pattern means target file is either old version or new version, never partial

userStories.notes:
Reuse anchors:
- `src/db/connection.rs::open_and_migrate` (line 58) â€” the implicit DB-creation path every command goes through today. Calling this explicitly is what makes init_project idempotent and gives the operator a clear entry point.
- `src/loop_engine/env.rs::ensure_directories` (~line 85) â€” `.task-mgr/` scaffolding.
- `src/loop_engine/project_config.rs::write_default_model` (line 180) â€” atomic write that preserves unknown fields via `serde_json::Value`. The pattern (read as Value, mutate as map, serialize, atomic tempfile rename) is what to copy.
- `src/loop_engine/project_config.rs::read_project_config` (line 163) â€” defaults-on-missing-or-invalid behavior.
- `src/commands/models/ensure_default.rs` â€” picker invocation when no model is resolved.
- `src/commands/doctor/setup_fixes.rs::fix_generate_claude_md` (line 263) â€” the existing 'check exists then template-write' idempotent pattern; init_project's config-write should follow it.

Learning [1093]: file-writing fix functions guard with symlink check first. Apply if init_project ever decides to overwrite .task-mgr/ contents (it shouldn't).

Learning [1424]: 'task-mgr init requires --from-json' â€” this learning becomes INVALID after this PR. REVIEW-001 will invalidate it via `task-mgr learn --invalidate 1424`.
qualityDimensions: Idempotent (running twice = same result; no file mtimes change on second run unless content changes)
Preserves existing config.json keys including unknown ones â€” uses Value-based roundtrip, not strongly-typed serialize
Never touches PRD JSON (no `parse_prd`, no `tasks/` glob, no prefix logic)
Picker fires only on TTY (matches CLAUDE.md line 63 contract)
userStories.title: Add project-level init_project function

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `task-mgr enhance agents --create --target /tmp/fresh.md --profile workflow` exits 0; /tmp/fresh.md exists with `<!-- TASK_MGR:BEGIN -->` and `<!-- TASK_MGR:END -->` and the workflow content between them
Unit test: run `enhance agents --target X` twice on a file containing user content before and after the marker block; second run produces byte-identical file (idempotency)
Unit test: file with prelude + marker block + postlude â†’ after `enhance agents` with new content, prelude/postlude unchanged byte-for-byte; marker contents replaced
Unit test: neither CLAUDE.md nor AGENTS.md present in CWD, `--create` not set â†’ exit 2; stderr lists both skipped
Unit test: `enhance strip` on file with markers + user content â†’ markers and block removed; user content preserved
Unit test: `enhance show --profile workflow` prints to stdout; no file writes (verify via mtime on any candidate file)
Unit test: `full` profile strictly contains `workflow` profile content (use length comparison + grep for a workflow-only token)
Unit test: unbalanced markers (BEGIN without END) in target â†’ returns Err; exit 2; stderr names the file
Negative: grep `src/commands/enhance/` for `std::fs::write` returns zero hits â€” all writes through `marker_splice::write_atomic`
Negative: must NOT modify any file path not listed in `--target` or the default-targets resolution result
Negative: `enhance show` and `enhance agents --dry-run` must NOT produce any file mtime changes (test via before/after stat)
CONTRACT: `EnhanceCommand` variant field names + types match what `src/main.rs` dispatch destructures â€” verify by `cargo check` after wiring
CONTRACT: `splice_block(current, "<!-- TASK_MGR:BEGIN -->", "<!-- TASK_MGR:END -->", new_block)` is the only marker-manipulation path; grep enhance/ for any other splice or `marker_begin` literal
Failure mode: target file path resolution fails (e.g., parent dir doesn't exist) â†’ enhance reports per-target error in EnhanceResult; other targets still attempted
userStories.description:
Add a new top-level command `task-mgr enhance` with three subcommands:
- `agents [--target <path>...] [--dry-run] [--create] [--profile workflow|full]` â€” writes the task-mgr-managed block (marker-fenced `<!-- TASK_MGR:BEGIN -->` ... `<!-- TASK_MGR:END -->`) into CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md (defaults; override with `--target`).
- `show [--profile workflow|full]` â€” prints the rendered block to stdout. No file writes.
- `strip [--target <path>...] [--dry-run]` â€” removes the marker block and its markers from target files.

Default targets when `--target` is empty: `./CLAUDE.md` AND `./AGENTS.md`. Each is touched only if it exists OR `--create` is set; otherwise skipped with a stderr note.

Profiles (stored as `&'static str` consts in templates.rs):
- `workflow` â€” task-mgr CLI cheat sheet (show, list, next, add --stdin, set-status), mid-loop sync pattern (`--append --update-existing`), `<task-status>` tag reference, spawn-fixup PRD-targeting gotcha, pointer to `task-mgr learn` / `recall` / `tm-decisions`. Mirrors Â§3a of the user-global CLAUDE.md, project-scoped.
- `full` â€” `workflow` + general LLM-coding guidelines (Think Before Coding, Simplicity First, Surgical Changes, Goal-Driven Execution). The full guideline text is provided in the user's request and goes verbatim into the const.

DO:
- Create `src/commands/enhance/{mod.rs, templates.rs, tests.rs}` (3 files).
- Define `EnhanceCommand` enum in `src/cli/commands.rs` with the three variants above.
- Wire `Commands::Enhance { cmd: EnhanceCommand }` and dispatch arms in `src/main.rs`.
- ALL file writes go through `task_mgr::util::marker_splice::write_atomic` (FEAT-001) â€” never `std::fs::write`.
- ALL marker manipulation uses `task_mgr::util::marker_splice::splice_block` (FEAT-001).
- Idempotent: running `enhance agents` twice on the same file produces byte-identical output.
- Return an EnhanceResult struct (per-target: name, action_taken, error) implementing TextFormattable for `--format json` support, modeled on SetupFix (Learning [1075]).

DO NOT:
- Auto-invoke enhance from any other command in this task (FEAT-005 wires the `--enhance` flag on `init`).
- Touch any file outside the resolved target list.
- Use format-string interpolation in templates â€” they are static `&'static str` for v1. Project-specific interpolation is deferred per Plan Â§9.
- Modify content outside the marker pair â€” splice_block guarantees this; do not introduce code paths that bypass it.
- Print the rendered block to stdout from `agents` (use `show` for that, or `--dry-run`).

Edge cases (each must have a test):
- CLAUDE.md missing, `--create` not set â†’ stderr 'skipping CLAUDE.md (not found; pass --create to create)'; exit 0 if any target succeeded, else 2.
- CLAUDE.md missing, `--create` set â†’ file created with marker block at start (after optional empty line).
- CLAUDE.md exists without markers â†’ block appended at end (newline separation).
- CLAUDE.md exists WITH markers â†’ block contents replaced; rest of file byte-identical.
- Both CLAUDE.md AND AGENTS.md exist (default targets) â†’ both updated.
- `--target docs/custom.md` (single explicit) â†’ only that file is touched; defaults NOT processed.
- `--dry-run` â†’ render to stdout via `splice_block` simulation; no writes; no exit-2 even if files missing.
- Unbalanced markers in target (BEGIN without END) â†’ return Err with file path + position; exit 2.
- `strip` on file without markers â†’ no-op; print 'no marker block found in <file>'; exit 0.

Known-bad to guard against:
- A naive impl that uses `std::fs::write` for the final commit (torn writes on crash). Grep verifies write_atomic is the only write path.
- An impl that replaces the entire file body instead of just the markers (would pass a test that only checks 'markers present' but is wrong).
edgeCases: CLAUDE.md missing, no --create: skip + stderr; exit 2 if all targets skipped, else 0
CLAUDE.md missing, --create set: file created with block
File exists without markers: append block at end
File exists with markers: replace contents between markers
Both default targets present: both updated
Single --target specified: only that file touched (defaults skipped)
--dry-run: no writes; render to stdout
Unbalanced markers in target: error with file path; exit 2
strip on no-markers file: no-op + informational stderr

failureModes.cause: write_atomic fails on one target (permission denied) while another succeeds
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Per-target error captured in EnhanceResult; remaining targets still processed; exit code reflects partial failure (2 if any failed; 0 only if all succeeded)

failureModes.cause: Unbalanced markers in existing CLAUDE.md (user manually edited)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Return Err with file path + line of unbalanced marker; do not attempt to 'fix' or guess

userStories.notes:
Module layout mirrors `src/commands/models/` (mod.rs + handlers.rs + types.rs + tests.rs pattern). Reuse anchors:
- `task_mgr::util::marker_splice::splice_block` and `::write_atomic` (FEAT-001) â€” the ONLY marker + write path.
- `src/commands/doctor/setup_fixes.rs::fix_generate_claude_md` (line 263) â€” the existing CLAUDE.md template precedent; treat as design ancestor.
- `src/commands/curate/output.rs` or `src/commands/models/handlers.rs` for the TextFormattable pattern (handlers.rs:54-60).
- `src/commands/curate/types.rs` for the per-action result struct pattern (mirrors EnhanceResult shape).

Workflow profile content (from Â§3a of user-global CLAUDE.md, project-scoped):
- CLI cheat sheet: `task-mgr show <id>`, `task-mgr list`, `task-mgr next`, `echo {...} | task-mgr add --stdin`, `task-mgr set-status <id> <status>`, `<task-status>X:done</task-status>` etc.
- Mid-loop sync warning: NEVER run bare `task-mgr init --from-json X` on a running effort; always pair with `--append --update-existing`. Note: post this PR, `task-mgr loop init X --append --update-existing` is the canonical form (the shim still works indefinitely).
- humanReviewOutcome pattern for CLARIFY tasks.
- Spawn-fixup PRD-targeting: pass `--depended-on-by <milestone-id>` or `--from-json tasks/<correct-prd>.json` when spawning ad-hoc fixups (CODE-FIX, WIRE-FIX, IMPL-FIX, REFACTOR-N).
- Pointer to `task-mgr learn` / `recall` / `tm-decisions` for learnings and decisions.

Full profile = workflow + verbatim copy of the user's LLM-coding guidelines (Think Before Coding, Simplicity First, Surgical Changes, Goal-Driven Execution â€” the 4-section content from the user's request).

Learning [1093]: idempotent guard pattern for file-writing fix functions â€” apply symlink check before write_atomic.
Learning [1075]: SetupFix struct (name/description/applied/error) â€” EnhanceResult mirrors this shape so the existing TextFormattable + output_result formatters work.
Learning [1405]: adding a subcommand variant requires changes across 6+ files (cli/commands.rs, commands/<name>/mod.rs, commands/<name>/types.rs, commands/<name>/output.rs, handlers.rs if present, main.rs). Check all of these are updated.
qualityDimensions: Marker-fenced: content outside markers is byte-identical before/after (splice_block guarantees)
Idempotent: second run produces same file content (mtime may change due to atomic rename, but byte content matches)
Atomic writes only: no `std::fs::write` anywhere in enhance/
Templates are static `&'static str` consts â€” no runtime interpolation
EnhanceResult per-target â€” one target failing doesn't abort the others
userStories.title: Add `task-mgr enhance` command (Agents/Show/Strip)

acceptanceCriteria: Unit test (parsing): `task-mgr loop init tasks/foo.json --append --update-existing` parses as `Commands::Loop { cmd: Some(LoopCommand::Init { prd_file, append: true, update_existing: true, .. }), prd_file: None, .. }`
Unit test (parsing): `task-mgr loop run tasks/foo.json --yes --hours 0.5` parses as `Commands::Loop { cmd: Some(LoopCommand::Run { prd_file, yes: true, hours: Some(0.5), .. }), .. }`
Unit test (parsing, flat-form shim): `task-mgr loop tasks/foo.json --yes` parses with `cmd: None, prd_file: Some("tasks/foo.json"), yes: true`
Unit test (dispatch, flat-form): on flat-form parse, dispatch synthesizes LoopCommand::Run with identical fields and emits stderr containing 'DEPRECATED'
Unit test (parsing): `task-mgr batch init '*.json'` parses as `Commands::Batch { cmd: Some(BatchCommand::Init { patterns: ["*.json"], .. }), .. }`
Unit test (parsing, flat-form): `task-mgr batch '*.json' --yes` parses with `cmd: None, patterns: ["*.json"], yes: true`

All 27 existing Loop parsing tests in src/cli/tests.rs (lines 2191-2472) either survive verbatim (assertions over the flat form via cmd=None) OR are rewritten to LoopCommand::Run patterns â€” assertion content preserved either way
All 10 existing Batch parsing tests (lines 2549-2666) similarly survive or are rewritten
Negative: dispatch must NOT call into `commands::init::init()` from any Run arm â€” only Init arms call init()
Negative: canonical `loop run` / `batch run` must NOT emit deprecation stderr (verify via captured stderr in test)
Negative: `commands::init::init()` signature unchanged â€” `git diff src/commands/init/mod.rs` shows only addition of init_project (from FEAT-002), no changes to existing init()
Failure mode: `task-mgr loop tasks/foo.json run` (both flat positional AND subcommand) â†’ clap errors at parse time (`args_conflicts_with_subcommands`)
CONTRACT: dispatch for LoopCommand::Init and BatchCommand::Init both call `commands::init::init(&dir, &paths, force, append, update_existing, dry_run, prefix_mode)` â€” same fn, same arg shape

Learning [1069]: after the refactor, `cargo build` succeeds with zero exhaustive-match errors â€” grep `src/cli/tests.rs` for `Commands::Loop {` and `Commands::Batch {` post-change to confirm all matches reflect the new shape
userStories.description:
Reshape `Commands::Loop` and `Commands::Batch` from flat variants into parent-with-optional-subcommand using clap's `args_conflicts_with_subcommands` + `subcommand_negates_reqs` pattern. The parents keep all their existing fields as deprecated companions (positional becomes `Option<...>`) so `task-mgr loop tasks/foo.json --yes` still parses; dispatch synthesizes `LoopCommand::Run { ... }` and emits stderr deprecation.

Add subcommand enums:
- `LoopCommand::Init { prd_file: PathBuf, force, append, update_existing, dry_run, prefix, no_prefix }` â€” calls existing `commands::init::init()` with `&[prd_file]`.
- `LoopCommand::Run { prd_file: PathBuf, prompt_file, yes, hours, verbose, no_worktree, external_repo, cleanup_worktree, parallel }` â€” calls existing loop runner.
- `BatchCommand::Init { patterns: Vec<String>, force, append, update_existing, dry_run, prefix, no_prefix }` â€” calls existing `init()` with globbed paths.
- `BatchCommand::Run { patterns, max_iterations, yes, keep_worktrees, chain, parallel }` â€” calls existing batch runner.

Dispatch logic (per parent in src/main.rs):
1. `cmd: Some(child)` â†’ handle child directly.
2. `cmd: None && (positional present)` (Loop: `prd_file.is_some()`; Batch: `!patterns.is_empty()`) â†’ `eprintln!('DEPRECATED: prefer `task-mgr {loop|batch} run ...`. The flat form will continue to work indefinitely.')`; synthesize child from flat fields; dispatch.
3. `cmd: None && no positional` â†’ print help; exit 2.

DO:
- Apply `#[command(args_conflicts_with_subcommands = true, subcommand_negates_reqs = true)]` to the Loop/Batch parent structs (or the clap-4 equivalent attribute names).
- Update EVERY pattern match over `Commands::Loop { .. }` / `Commands::Batch { .. }` in `src/cli/tests.rs` â€” there are ~37 of them. Learning [1069]: exhaustive matches break when fields change.
- Add NEW parsing tests for the canonical forms: `task-mgr loop init <prd>`, `task-mgr loop run <prd>`, `task-mgr batch init <pat>`, `task-mgr batch run <pat>`.
- Add NEW parsing tests for the flat-form shim: `task-mgr loop <prd>` parses with `cmd: None, prd_file: Some(...)`; dispatch wraps into Run.
- Use `clap::CommandFactory` (Learning [160]) to programmatically introspect the new subcommand tree in tests, rather than spawning subprocesses.

DO NOT:
- Change `commands::init::init()` signature or behavior. It's called from THREE places after this PR (top-level shim, LoopCommand::Init, BatchCommand::Init); a signature change breaks all three.
- Change behavior of the existing loop runner or batch runner. LoopCommand::Run / BatchCommand::Run just call the same underlying functions with the same args as today's flat dispatch.
- Drop any flag from the flat-form fallback. `--yes`, `--hours`, `--prompt-file`, `--no-worktree`, `--external-repo`, `--cleanup-worktree`, `--parallel` (Loop) and `--max-iterations`, `--keep-worktrees`, `--chain`, `--parallel` (Batch) all still parse on the flat form.
- Hide the deprecation message â€” `eprintln!` always (no `--quiet` opt-out in v1).
- Pattern-match `Commands::Loop { prd_file, ... }` outside the dispatch arm. Only main.rs handles the flat shape; tests update to nested forms.

Edge cases (each must have a test):
- `task-mgr loop` (zero args, no subcommand) â†’ print help; exit 2.
- `task-mgr loop init` (subcommand but missing required prd_file) â†’ clap usage error; exit 2.
- `task-mgr loop tasks/foo.json` (deprecated positional) â†’ stderr deprecation + dispatch to LoopCommand::Run with prd_file=Some('tasks/foo.json').
- `task-mgr loop run tasks/foo.json` (canonical) â†’ no deprecation; same behavior as today's flat form.
- `task-mgr batch '*.json'` (deprecated positional) â†’ stderr deprecation + dispatch to BatchCommand::Run with patterns=['*.json'].
- `task-mgr batch init '*.json' '*.json'` (multi pattern on init) â†’ parses fine; init() called with globbed paths.
- File literally named `init` passed as positional to deprecated form (`task-mgr loop init` where `init` is a real file â€” collision with subcommand) â†’ clap resolves as subcommand; user must use `task-mgr loop run init` or `task-mgr loop run ./init`. Document this in --help.

Known-bad to guard against:
- A naive impl that uses two separate enums for nested vs flat (would double dispatch code paths; instead use Option<LoopCommand> with the flat fields on the parent).
- An impl that emits the deprecation notice from the canonical `loop run` path (would spam stderr on every iteration).
edgeCases: Zero args (`task-mgr loop`): print help, exit 2
Subcommand missing required (`task-mgr loop init` without prd): clap error, exit 2
Flat-form (`task-mgr loop tasks/foo.json`): deprecation + dispatch to Run
Canonical (`task-mgr loop run tasks/foo.json`): no deprecation; same as old flat
Multi-pattern batch init: globbed paths threaded to init()
File literally named `init` collides with subcommand: documented escape `loop run ./init`

failureModes.expectedBehavior: cargo build fails with non-exhaustive match error pointing at the unupdated test; fix in place
Use subcommand_negates_reqs in addition; verify in a minimal prototype before refactoring the whole file
clap rejects at parse time per args_conflicts_with_subcommands semantics; exit 2 with usage hint

userStories.notes:
Reuse anchors:
- `src/cli/commands.rs:66-97` (current Init), `:809-861` (current Loop), `:895-936` (current Batch) â€” the existing field set to preserve as deprecated companions.
- `src/main.rs:116` (Init dispatch), `:803` (Loop), `:869` (Batch) â€” the existing dispatch arms; refactor each to handle nested + flat.
- `commands::init::init` (`src/commands/init/mod.rs:182`) â€” call this from both LoopCommand::Init and BatchCommand::Init (with `&[prd_file]` and `&globbed[..]` respectively). Same signature, same behavior, two call sites instead of one.
- Existing tests `src/cli/tests.rs:2191-2472` (Loop, ~27 patterns) and `:2549-2666` (Batch, ~10 patterns) â€” update each pattern.

Learning [1069]: NEW fields on clap enum variants break exhaustive matches in tests. Every `Commands::Loop { .. }` and `Commands::Batch { .. }` match â€” including those in `src/cli/tests.rs` that test other parsing variants â€” must be updated. Use `cargo build` failure output as a checklist.

Learning [1405]: adding a subcommand variant typically touches 6+ files. Same applies here for Loop and Batch nesting â€” verify cli/commands.rs + main.rs + cli/tests.rs + any handlers if extracted.

Learning [160]: use `clap::CommandFactory` to introspect subcommands in tests rather than subprocess-based testing â€” faster + deterministic.
qualityDimensions: Flat-form shim works for every existing flag combination â€” no flag dropped
Canonical form (loop run / batch run) suppresses deprecation noise â€” operators who migrate don't see warnings
Existing init() function signature + behavior unchanged
Pattern-match updates in src/cli/tests.rs preserve assertion content â€” only the wrapping pattern changes
Tests use clap::CommandFactory for subcommand introspection (no subprocess spawns)
userStories.title: Nest Loop and Batch subcommands with flat-form shim

acceptanceCriteria: Unit test (parsing): `task-mgr init` parses with `from_json: vec![], enhance: false`
Unit test (parsing): `task-mgr init --enhance` parses with `enhance: true`
Unit test (parsing): `task-mgr init --from-json X.json` parses (deprecated shim form still accepted)
Behavior test: `task-mgr init` in fresh tmpdir â†’ init_project called; .task-mgr/ exists; stderr contains 'enhance agents' hint
Behavior test: `task-mgr init --enhance` in fresh tmpdir â†’ init_project AND handle_agents called; CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md exist with TASK_MGR markers
Behavior test: `task-mgr init --force` (no from_json) â†’ exit 2; stderr explains 'rm -rf .task-mgr/'

GOLDEN-FILE TEST (KEY): identical fixture PRD; run `init --from-json fixture.json --append --update-existing` on tmpdir A; run `loop init fixture.json --append --update-existing` on tmpdir B (both had init_project run first); assert `task-mgr list --format json --prefix <prefix>` output is byte-identical between A and B (after canonical JSON sort)

Behavior test: `task-mgr init --from-json X.json` emits stderr DEPRECATED notice + runs picker (verify by setting up a fresh dir with no resolved model and asserting `.task-mgr/config.json` has defaultModel after the shim path, but NOT after `loop init X.json` directly)
Behavior test: `task-mgr init --from-json X --enhance` â†’ enhance ignored; stderr note explains; PRD still imported
Negative: `commands::init::init()` signature unchanged â€” `git diff` confirms
Negative: `loop init` and `batch init` direct invocations do NOT fire the picker (no defaultModel written by them)
Negative: `task-mgr init --force` (no from_json) must NOT touch `.task-mgr/` â€” exit before any DB or config write
CONTRACT: shim dispatch passes the same `&[PathBuf]` to `init()` that LoopCommand::Init / BatchCommand::Init dispatch does â€” single call site shape, two routing paths
Failure mode: if init_project fails (e.g., permission denied), shim path also fails BEFORE any PRD import begins (verify ordering)
userStories.description:
Reshape `Commands::Init` so `from_json: Vec<PathBuf>` becomes optional (was `required = true`). Behavior split in src/main.rs dispatch:
- Empty `from_json` â†’ call `init_project(dir)` (FEAT-002). Print stderr hint: 'Initialized .task-mgr/. Run `task-mgr enhance agents` to add task-mgr workflow guidance to CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md.'
- Non-empty `from_json` â†’ stderr 'DEPRECATED: `task-mgr init --from-json X` keeps working indefinitely but the canonical form is `task-mgr loop init X` (single PRD) or `task-mgr batch init <glob>...` (multi).' THEN run `init_project(dir)` first (fires the picker per Plan Â§7) THEN dispatch the PRD payload via LoopCommand::Init (when N=1) or BatchCommand::Init (when N>1) â€” both call into existing `commands::init::init()` (PRD import code unchanged).

Add `#[arg(long, default_value_t = false)] enhance: bool` on `Commands::Init`:
- `task-mgr init --enhance` â†’ after init_project, also run `commands::enhance::handle_agents(dir, vec![], false, true, "full")` so both CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md get created/updated with the full profile.
- `--enhance` is silently IGNORED when `--from-json` is non-empty (shim path doesn't auto-enhance; print stderr note 'note: --enhance is ignored on the deprecated --from-json path; run `task-mgr init --enhance` separately').

Add `task-mgr init --force` (no `from_json`) â†’ reject with stderr: 'Project-level init has no destructive form. To reset, `rm -rf .task-mgr/` and re-run.' Exit 2. Per Plan Â§6.

DO:
- Make `from_json: Vec<PathBuf>` optional (drop `required = true`). All companion flags (force, append, update_existing, dry_run, prefix, no_prefix) stay but are only honored when from_json is non-empty.
- Add `--enhance` flag.
- Implement the dispatch split in src/main.rs (project vs shim vs reject).
- Add a GOLDEN-FILE equivalence test in `src/commands/init/tests.rs`: set up two identical fixture DBs (or two fixture dirs); run `init --from-json X --append --update-existing` on dir A and `loop init X --append --update-existing` on dir B (both already have init_project run); assert row+relationship+file equality.
- Update `src/cli/tests.rs` Init parsing tests (lines 16-74) â€” split into 'new no-arg form' and 'deprecated shim form' groups.

DO NOT:
- Change row-level behavior of `commands::init::init()` â€” same fn, same args, two dispatch paths.
- Drop the model picker side effect on the shim path. Operators who run `task-mgr init --from-json X` today get the picker; the shim preserves that by running `init_project(dir)` BEFORE the PRD dispatch.
- Fire the picker from `loop init` or `batch init` direct invocations â€” those are PRD-scoped, not project-scoped.
- Auto-run enhance when `--enhance` is NOT set. Bare `task-mgr init` only scaffolds + prints hint.
- Forget to update `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` text via FEAT-006 â€” that's the operator-facing doc.

Edge cases (each must have a test):
- `task-mgr init` (no args) â†’ init_project + hint to stderr; exit 0.
- `task-mgr init --enhance` â†’ init_project + handle_agents(create=true, profile=full); both CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md created/updated.
- `task-mgr init --force` (no from_json) â†’ exit 2; stderr explains alternative.
- `task-mgr init --from-json X.json` (single PRD) â†’ init_project (picker) + LoopCommand::Init { prd_file: X, .. } dispatch path.
- `task-mgr init --from-json A.json --from-json B.json` (multi PRD) â†’ init_project + BatchCommand::Init { patterns: [A.json, B.json], .. } dispatch.
- `task-mgr init --from-json X --enhance` â†’ enhance silently skipped; stderr note; PRD imported.

Known-bad to guard against:
- An impl that calls `init()` directly from the shim instead of routing through LoopCommand::Init / BatchCommand::Init dispatch â€” would drift from canonical path semantics. The golden-file test catches this.
- An impl that runs init_project AFTER PRD dispatch on the shim path â€” would skip the picker for operators who don't have a model resolved yet.
edgeCases: No args: init_project + hint
--enhance: init_project + enhance agents (create + full profile)
--force (no from_json): reject with stderr; exit 2
--from-json X.json (single): shim â†’ init_project â†’ LoopCommand::Init dispatch
--from-json A.json --from-json B.json (multi): shim â†’ init_project â†’ BatchCommand::Init dispatch
--from-json X --enhance: enhance ignored; stderr note; PRD imported

failureModes.cause: init_project fails on shim path (permission denied, disk full)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Return Err BEFORE any PRD import; .task-mgr/ may be partial but no PRD data corrupted

failureModes.cause: Golden-file test reveals shim vs canonical drift (different row state)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Bisect: most likely the shim path skipped a step or called init() with different args; fix the dispatch to mirror LoopCommand::Init exactly

failureModes.cause: Picker fires on `loop init` direct invocation (regression)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Pin: picker is invoked from init_project only; loop init / batch init must NOT call init_project; they call init() (PRD import) directly

userStories.notes:
Reuse anchors:
- `init_project(dir)` from FEAT-002 â€” project-level scaffold + picker.
- `commands::enhance::handle_agents(dir, target, dry_run, create, profile)` from FEAT-003 â€” called with `(dir, vec![], false, true, "full")` for the --enhance convenience.
- `commands::init::init(dir, &json_files, force, append, update_existing, dry_run, prefix_mode)` (src/commands/init/mod.rs:182) â€” UNCHANGED; called from LoopCommand::Init (single) and BatchCommand::Init (multi) dispatch arms.
- Picker helper in `src/commands/models/ensure_default.rs` â€” invoked from init_project per Plan Â§7.

Learning [1424] becomes INVALID after this PR: 'task-mgr init requires --from-json' is no longer true. REVIEW-001 invalidates it via `task-mgr learn --invalidate 1424`.

Golden-file test design: the cleanest implementation uses two tmpdirs with identical PRD JSON; runs both code paths; queries `task-mgr list --format json --prefix <auto-generated-prefix>` from each; sorts and compares. Alternative: direct SQL `SELECT * FROM tasks ORDER BY id` from both DBs and assert row equality.

Learning [1505]: FK delete order matters in --force. We're NOT changing --force behavior (it stays on the loop init / batch init paths and on the deprecated shim) so this learning applies to verifying that path still works post-refactor.

Learning [1685]: clap env var shadowing in tests. The new --enhance flag has no env binding so this doesn't apply, but be careful with future env-bound flags.
qualityDimensions: Golden-file equivalence: shim path produces identical DB state as canonical path â€” enforced by test, not just convention
Picker timing on shim path preserves today's operator UX (picker fires before PRD import)

--enhance is opt-in only (no surprise file mutations outside .task-mgr/)
Project-level --force is rejected with a clear actionable error message

userStories.description:
Run `cargo run --bin gen-docs` to regenerate `.claude/commands/tasks.md` reflecting the new canonical commands (`task-mgr init` project-level, `task-mgr loop init` / `task-mgr batch init` for PRD import, `task-mgr enhance agents` for instruction file management). Update hand-edited operator docs. Add a 'Deprecation policy' note in CLAUDE.md (project) so future-self knows the shim lives indefinitely.

Files to edit:
- `README.md` lines 64, 328, 465 (mentions of `task-mgr init --from-json`).
- `CLAUDE.md` (project root) lines 63 (interactive picker description), 75 (PRD-JSON-in-two-places).
- `.claude/commands/tasks.md` â€” regenerated by gen-docs.

DO:
- Run `cargo run --bin gen-docs` and commit the regenerated `.claude/commands/tasks.md` in the same commit as the doc edits.
- Update README sections that show `task-mgr init --from-json` to show BOTH the new canonical form AND a brief note about the indefinite shim.
- Add a new README subsection 'Project-level init vs PRD-level init' so first-time readers see the distinction.
- Add a new README subsection 'Agent instructions: `task-mgr enhance`' covering the new command with one example each for `agents`, `show`, `strip`.
- In CLAUDE.md (project), update line ~63 to describe the new picker behavior: fires from project-level `task-mgr init` AND from the deprecated `init --from-json X` shim path.
- Update CLAUDE.md (project) line ~75's worktree note to use `task-mgr loop init` syntax instead of `task-mgr init --from-json`.
- Add a new CLAUDE.md subsection 'Deprecation policy' stating the shim is permanent; cite Plan Â§11.

DO NOT:
- Hand-edit `.claude/commands/tasks.md` â€” it's generated by gen-docs. Run the binary, commit the diff.
- Update `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (user-global) from this task â€” that file is outside the repo and the shim makes it lag-tolerant.
- Update `mw-datalake/docs/TASK_MGR.md` from this task â€” separate repo; out of scope.
- Delete existing `task-mgr init --from-json` examples â€” annotate them as deprecated; the shim is permanent so the examples remain valid.
- Modify `.task-mgr/`, `tasks/`, or other state directories.

Edge cases:
- gen-docs regeneration produces a diff in `.claude/commands/tasks.md` â†’ commit it; the CI gate `gen-docs --check` is what enforces freshness.
- README's existing 'deprecated form' example is identical to new canonical form (rare) â†’ no annotation needed.
- CLAUDE.md line 63 or 75 has drifted from this task's expected text â†’ grep for the relevant section header and update the closest match.

Known-bad to guard against:
- A doc update that removes the `--from-json` examples entirely. Operators still use that form; the shim is permanent; the examples stay (with deprecation annotations).
- A doc update that fails the `gen-docs --check` CI gate. Run `cargo run --bin gen-docs -- --check` locally before committing.

Learning [951, 961, 996]: writes to `.claude/commands/` are denied in Claude Code don't-ask permission mode. This task runs in INTERACTIVE mode (the loop default for this PR), so it can write. If running in headless CI, the gen-docs binary writes the file natively (not Claude) so this is fine.

Gotcha: gen-docs writes via the `splice_block` and `write_atomic` helpers that FEAT-001 moved to `src/util/marker_splice.rs`. Make sure FEAT-001 is complete before running gen-docs here (the build will fail otherwise).

userStories.description:
Review all implementation across FEAT-001..006 for DRY violations, complexity, coupling, and pattern consistency.

DO: Check for duplicated logic (e.g. marker handling in multiple places, dispatch synthesis in main.rs that should be a helper), functions >30 lines, tight coupling between modules. Look specifically at:
- src/main.rs dispatch arms â€” did the Loop/Batch/Init shim logic grow into a function that should be extracted?
- src/commands/enhance/mod.rs â€” is target-resolution logic reusable, or hardcoded inside handle_agents?
- src/commands/init/mod.rs â€” is init_project sharing helpers with init() cleanly, or duplicating directory-creation code?
- src/cli/tests.rs â€” are the ~40 pattern-update commits leaving behind dead test helpers?

userStories.notes: If issues found: `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` for each (priority 50-97) â€” atomic DB+JSON sync, no manual JSON edit. userStories.description:
Review all implementation for quality, security, integration wiring, and documentation. RUN THE FULL QUALITY GATE (unscoped test suite).

DO:
- Check every new function in enhance/ and init/init_project for error handling (no unwrap() in production paths; all DB/file errors map_err'd with context).
- Verify integration wiring: `task-mgr enhance` reachable from `task-mgr --help`; `task-mgr loop init` and `task-mgr batch init` reachable from their parents' help; `task-mgr init --enhance` documented in --help.
- Verify Learning [1069] regression: grep `src/cli/tests.rs` for `Commands::Loop {` and `Commands::Batch {` â€” every match reflects the new variant shape (cmd + optional flat fields), no stale references to the old required-positional shape.
- Run the FULL `cargo test` (no scope), `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and `cargo run --bin gen-docs -- --check`. Fix every failure including pre-existing ones (escape hatch: >~12 unrelated failures â†’ spawn a FIX-xxx with the failures listed and BLOCK).
- Invalidate Learning [1424] via `task-mgr learn --invalidate 1424` since 'task-mgr init requires --from-json' is no longer true after FEAT-005.

- Verify the shim path with a manual smoke: in a fresh tmpdir, run `task-mgr init --from-json <fixture>.json` and confirm stderr deprecation appears + .task-mgr/ scaffolds + PRD imports.
